items.title: Scientists expected a black hole but found a neutrino factory powered by stars
items.summary: A distant galaxy nicknamed Shadow Blaster may have revealed a surprising source of cosmic neutrinos: extreme star formation instead of a supermassive black hole. The discovery suggests that hidden, dust-filled starburst galaxies could account for a significant fraction of the Universe’s high-energy

items.title: Future astronauts could walk across rocks from deep inside the Moon
items.summary: A colossal ancient collision may have left some of the Moon’s deepest secrets surprisingly close to future Artemis landing sites. By recreating the impact that formed the giant South Pole-Aitken basin—the Moon’s largest and oldest crater—scientists found that a low-angle strike from a large, iron-co

items.title: Scientists discover neurons must break their DNA to build the brain
items.summary: As newborn neurons make their way through the developing brain, they must squeeze through incredibly tight spaces to reach their final destinations. Researchers discovered that this physical journey routinely causes some of the most severe forms of DNA damage—double-strand breaks—yet the young brain

items.title: Butterfly that barely ages could help unlock longevity secrets
items.summary: Scientists discovered that Heliconius butterflies have evolved an extraordinary lifespan, living several times longer than closely related species. Even more surprising, some show little sign of physical decline as they age. Their unusual pollen-feeding lifestyle may play a role, but the research su

items.title: Researchers found a Wordle strategy that wins 99% of the time
items.summary: Researchers developed a Wordle-solving strategy that succeeds 99% of the time by focusing on information gain rather than likely answers.
